steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The left side ends with the noun “newts” as the object of a preposition. The right side begins with “for instance” (a parenthetical transitional element) and continues with the independent clause “you could encounter a key…”.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
Parenthetical phrases within a sentence are set off with commas.

Option D correctly uses a comma to set off the parenthetical phrase “for instance” from the preceding noun phrase.

The other options use incorrect punctuation: a colon and semicolon each require specific clause structures that don't apply here, and a period creates a sentence fragment.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
